fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Missouri Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Missouri - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity missouri, how to transfer occupational therapist license to missouri, occupational therapist compact missouri, missouri occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Missouri Advisory Commission for Occupational Therapy
board.mailing_address:
Missouri Board of Occupational Therapy
3605 Missouri Boulevard
P.O. Box 1335
Jefferson City, MO 65102

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Missouri.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create Portal Account & Submit Application
steps.description: Register for an account on the Missouri Division of Professional Registration Portal (bsd.pr.mo.gov) and complete the online application for Occupational Therapist licensure.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your accredited occupational therapy program (Bachelor's, Master's, or Doctoral) to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Missouri Board of Occupational Therapy.

steps.title: Submit NBCOT Certification
steps.description: Request an official score report or verification of certification directly from the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) to be sent to the Missouri Board of Occupational Therapy.

steps.title: Complete Missouri Jurisprudence Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the Missouri Jurisprudence Examination. Information and access to this exam are typically provided within the online application portal or on the Board's website.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Undergo a state and federal criminal background check. Follow the instructions provided by the Board for fingerprinting through the MACHS Fingerprint System.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the required application fee of $125 through the Missouri Division of Professional Registration Portal as part of your online application.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Missouri OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Missouri have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Missouri has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Missouri?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Missouri while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in Missouri
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Missouri, you must submit an online application through the Missouri Division of Professional Registration Portal.

official_links.portal_name: Missouri Division of Professional Registration Portal
official_links.registration_steps: 1. Navigate to the Missouri Division of Professional Registration Portal at https://bsd.pr.mo.gov/. 2. Click on 'New User' to register for an account using a valid email address. 3. Follow the prompts to complete your user profile and create login credentials. 4. Once registered and logged in, select the appropriate application for Occupational Therapist licensure.
